commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Pi as an optional autoreview engine across the shared autoreview skill docs, Python helper, and smoke harness allow-lists.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The changed behavior is validated by the contributor's copied terminal output for Pi dry-run and harness execution.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Remaining action is maintainer review of the new Pi engine security boundary and normal checks; there is no narrow automated repair left after the trust-denial fix.

target.title: feat(autoreview): add pi review engine
target.body:
## Summary
- add `pi` as a supported autoreview engine
- wire the helper to the Pi CLI with `--pi-bin`, Pi thinking levels, and read-only tool mode
- document Pi support and allow the smoke harnesses to accept `pi`

## Details
This uses the local `earendil-works/pi` repo as the reference for Pi's CLI contract. The autoreview helper now invokes `pi` in print mode with sessions, context files, extensions, skills, prompt templates, and themes disabled, and uses Pi's built-in read-only tools (`read,grep,find,ls`) when tools are enabled so review stays non-mutating and scoped to inspection.

## Validation
- `scripts/validate-skills`
- `ruby -c scripts/install-skills && ruby -c scripts/validate-skills`
- `bash -n skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness`
- `python3 -m py_compile skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness.py`
- `node --check skills/agent-transcript/scripts/agent-transcript`
- `node --test skills/agent-transcript/scripts/agent-transcript.test.mjs skills/session-viewer/scripts/session-viewer.test.ts`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --engine pi --thinking high --pi-bin pi --dry-run`
- `python3 skills/autoreview/scripts/test-review-harness.py --fixture malicious --engine pi`
target.author: coygeek
